context: Cloud-API frontier-judge re-eval (task #122 round 7) post iter-275 v8 engine promotion + iter-336 PHI source-scan lock-in. Companion to round-6 local-LLM run (composite 7.56 on n=5/6 Ollama Q4_K_M judges). Round-7 uses xAI Grok 4.1 fast-reasoning, Mistral Large, DeepSeek-Chat, Perplexity Sonar Pro, NVIDIA Nemotron Ultra 253B — frontier-tier reasoning models for higher-quality signal.

blocking_gaps_for_10_10.gap: Advisor attestation referenced but no direct link or signed review artifact provided in package.
No FDA Q-Sub draft or submission readiness package included.
Test count and CI green claimed but no badges, logs, or coverage reports visible in package.
No screenshots or live render; relies on text walkthrough despite live demo links.

strengths: Outstanding JUDGES.md with 5-minute audit, pinned artifacts, and full audit trail.
Core reproducibility via Q16.16 BitNet with cross-arch bit-identical replay and repro_hash.
Comprehensive FHIR R4 coverage (18 resources) and 21 CFR Part 11 audit tooling.
1371+ tests across engine/scripts with specific pinning and gates.
Clean Apache-2.0 with explicit patent grant and mind-mem ecosystem integration.

blocking_gaps_for_10_10.gap: Lacks peer-reviewed publication or preprint demonstrating novel CS contributions beyond BitNet b1.58 adaptation.
No direct comparison to Epic Detergent or OpenEvidence's proprietary safety layers in the submission materials.
Clinical advisor attestation lacks formal IRB or institutional endorsement for the validation cohort.
Hero animation (audit-chain ring) may distract from core value proposition in a 5-second scan.
Brand palette (Prompt Opinion orange) risks clashing with FDA's clinical aesthetic guidelines in production deployments.

strengths: Bit-identical Q16.16 reproducibility primitive with cross-arch replay (x86/ARM/RISC-V/Pi Zero/A100) and SHA-256 audit hashes — a standout for FDA SaMD defensibility.
100% recall on contraindicated/major drug pairs (44/44 + 4/4) with 0 false positives on negative-control cohort, validated by NPI-attributed clinical advisor.
21 CFR Part 11-compliant audit chain with Ed25519 signing, SHA-256 predecessor hashing, and unit-tested export shape.
Apache-2.0 + explicit patent grant (§3) with no buried licensing, enabling immediate production deployment.
1371 tests (1310+ passed) with CI reproducibility-manifest checks, including 9-test BitNet bundle integrity pinning and 7-test negative-control cohort integrity.
FHIR R4 coverage (18 resource types) with 30 synthetic Synthea patients and 47 CMS Luhn-valid NPIs, demonstrating real-world interoperability.
Typed runtime invariants (21 in JointMemoryFederation.flow.mind) enforcing PHI gates, Ed25519 signing, and X25519 sealing — not just policy documentation.

JUDGES.md provides a 60-second scan, 5-minute audit, and full audit trail with traceability from dashboard claims to checked-in artifacts (e.g., `docs/openevidence_cache.json` → `docs/pccp_eval_latest.json`).

blocking_gaps_for_10_10.gap: No novel CS contribution beyond integration of existing components; Q16.16 and BitNet are known techniques.
Bit-identical replay is novel but the overall pipeline architecture mirrors existing clinical decision support systems.
Single clinical advisor with no evidence of independent clinical validation or IRB review.
No contribution guidelines, issue templates, or community governance documented.
No evidence of 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature controls or full audit trail for user identity.
No live interactive demo; static HTML dashboard with no real-time API calls or user interaction.
No FDA Q-Sub draft or pre-submission documentation included.
No blocking gaps; license is clear and Apache-2.0 with patent grant.
No evidence of integration tests covering the full pipeline end-to-end with live APIs.
Dashboard is static HTML with no responsive design or accessibility compliance documented.

strengths: Bit-identical Q16.16 fixed-point replay across architectures is a strong FDA-defensibility primitive.
1371 passing tests with CI reproducibility manifest demonstrate rigorous engineering discipline.
Apache-2.0 with explicit patent grant enables production deployment without IP concerns.
Clinical advisor with valid NPI and attestation adds credibility beyond typical hackathon submissions.
Comprehensive FHIR R4 coverage with 18 resource types and 30 synthetic patients.
PHI discipline with typed runtime invariants and structured logging shows attention to compliance.
